title: acosta boissieri subsp. atlantica
intro: Acosta boissieri subsp. atlantica, also known as Atlantic Acosta, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a type of shrub native to the western coast of the Iberian Peninsula, Portugal, and southwestern Europe, typically found growing in rocky and sandy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acosta boissieri subsp. atlantica include its potential to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, and it is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture.
